proposed_features.description: The texts differ in expressing positive or negative feelings about the subject, which can separate favorable from unfavorable opinions.
Some texts emphasize emotional responses or feelings evoked, distinguishing those that highlight emotional engagement from those that do not.
Certain texts reference particular components like acting, soundtrack, or visuals, which can differentiate detailed critiques from more general statements.
The presence of strong positive or negative descriptive words (e.g., brilliant, terrible) can help distinguish enthusiastic from critical texts.
Some texts explicitly mention how engaging or boring the experience was, which can separate texts based on perceived entertainment value.
Certain texts conclude with a clear overall evaluation (e.g., masterpiece, not worth the time), which can distinguish conclusive opinions from descriptive ones.
Some texts comment on the complexity or pacing of the subject, differentiating those that critique structure from those that focus on content or emotion.
Texts that include personal reactions or feelings (e.g., 'I was bored') differ from those that remain more objective or descriptive.
Some texts contrast different elements (e.g., good acting but bad writing), which can separate nuanced critiques from uniformly positive or negative ones.
Texts vary in how specifically they discuss elements (e.g., naming acting, soundtrack) versus making broad statements, which can distinguish detailed reviews from general impressions.
